name: LeQuint Allen
headline: Pass-pro specialist needs a bigger backfield slice to matter
body:
Allen's rookie tape reads like a situational role, not a feature back: 16 games, just a 22.6% snap share, 23 carries for 94 yards (4.09 per pop) and 10 catches on 11 targets for 54 more. The efficiency markers underline the limited ceiling, with a -0.209 EPA per play, only 8.7% of runs going for explosive gains, and a meager 1.52 yards after contact per attempt. He found the end zone zero times and saw just six red-zone opportunities all year.

The value sits in dynasty patience rather than 2026 production. At 21, Allen earned trust on third downs for his pass protection, which keeps him on the field but caps the fantasy payoff since blocking does not score. Until his touch count climbs meaningfully, he projects as a waiver-wire flier in redraft. Dynasty managers can stash him cheaply and bet on the role expanding.
